design_philosophy.core_loop: Select wrestler with unique moveset → engage in one-on-one or multi-wrestler matches → chain grapples, strikes, and signature moves → defeat opponent via pin, submission, or knockout
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Learning opponent wrestler patterns and counter-timing execution in real-time combat
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Embody a favorite WWF professional wrestler and simulate televised wrestling matches with authentic roster and movesets
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Licensed wrestling sim built on the WWF Super WrestleMania engine; prioritizes wrestler authenticity (roster, move signatures, visual themes) over arcade accessibility. Assumes player interest in wrestling entertainment narrative context.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Match outcome determined by wrestler selection (move set variety and speed) and real-time grapple/strike timing execution against AI or human opponents

progression.unlock_mechanism: Wrestler roster selection available from outset; 12 playable wrestlers with distinct move sets (13th placeholder character 'Mystery' unused)
replayability_hooks: 12 distinct wrestler movesets encourage multiple playthroughs with different characters
Two-player versus mode with character asymmetry
Unused Mystery wrestler accessible via cheat codes suggests developer consideration of extended roster

world_building_techniques: Licensed WWF wrestler roster with authentic ring attire and visual identity
Titantron graphics displayed on victory (incomplete for unused Mystery character)
Wrestler selection screen includes biographies contextualizing roster
Theme music associated with each wrestler for entrance immersion

faction_indicators: WWF roster selection implies brand allegiance and era-specific wrestling identity (1994 era)
environmental_storytelling: Wrestling ring setting with crowd environment
Wrestler entrance Titantron displays establish in-universe presence
Match outcomes determine wrestler narrative momentum (championship progression implied but not documented)

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - WWF Raw SNES article)
notes: Available sources provide limited design philosophy detail. Wikipedia disambiguation page confirms game existence but offers no gameplay mechanics documentation. TCRF source documents developer (Sculptured Software), publisher (LJN), unused content (Mystery character, theme music), and engine lineage (WWF Super WrestleMania) but lacks difficulty settings, progression structure, and boss/match design patterns. Rating reduced to T2 qualitative due to sparse mechanic documentation.
